summary: Every system handling personal data must honour its data-protection obligations — retention limits, lawful consent, tracking rules, and data-subject rights — as a governed capability, with the concrete laws and numbers bound per repo at L3.

rules.rule: Declare, per class of personal data, a retention window set by the legal requirement for that type and jurisdiction, the archive destination, and the legal basis — past the window, archive cold rather than hard-delete, deleting only when a legal obligation requires it.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Record the lawful basis for data that requires it — who/what/when and the scope consented to — keep it withdrawable, and honour withdrawal downstream.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Run non-essential client-side tracking (cookies, pixels, fingerprinting, analytics) without the consent the jurisdiction requires — tracking is categorized strictly-necessary vs optional and consented where required first.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Be able to fulfil data-subject requests over the personal data held — access, rectification, erasure where legally due, portability, and objection/restriction — with erasure extending to copies and, where legally required, to the audit-trail as a governed, logged exception.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Each tenant/system declares its governing jurisdiction(s) — that declaration binds the concrete laws, retention windows, and DSR timelines at L3, and a multi-tenant system may carry different jurisdictions per tenant.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
